records.ety: [OE. justen, jousten, OF. jouster, jouster, joster, F. jouter, fr. L. juxta near to, nigh, from the root of jungere to join. See Join, and cf. Jostle.]

defs: To engage in mock combat on horseback, as two knights in the lists; to tilt.
quotes: For the whole army to joust and tourney.

defs: To engage in a competition involving one-to-one struggle with an opponent.

records.ety: [OE. juste, jouste, OF. juste, jouste, joste, F. joute. See Joust, v. i.]

defs: A tilting match; a mock combat on horseback between two knights in the lists or inclosed field.
quotes: Gorgeous knights at joust and tournament.

defs: Any competition involving one-to-one struggle with an opponent.
